speaker: MATT
raw_text: I would like you to roll initiative.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Also my Control Weather spell should still be active.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: So nature check? Druid check? What the shit is happening check?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Nurture check? Nature versus nurture check? 14?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 14. This is definitely not a natural wind. And as you start to get that dark feeling at the back of your head, you start noticing that some of the broken glass that is across the entirety of this beach being picked up by the wind. Swirling and congealing into a small whirlwind of broken glass and dust.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You watch as the wind begins to pick up into two other separate, smaller shapes, but that one central funnel continues to grow and grow, like this angry, waving tornado of broken--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to flip one of the boats over as a shield and hide behind it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: As a druid, would I recognize if this is an air elemental situation?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Ugh, why are they always intelligence--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: With advantage because it is possibly elemental-based.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Two of the entities that are coming out of this wind from the sides are definitely air elemental by nature. You're very familiar with this form of physicality. You've taken on this form of physicality. However, whatever this large, strange glass-shard-encompassed entity is, it's elemental in nature but you're not sure what it is. You've not encountered something of this form, at least on this plane.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: And with that, let's go ahead and roll some initiative, folks.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Just the three of them, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Does the wind seem strong enough that if I hopped up on my broom that I would get blown away?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay, I want to try doing that, then.

preceding_turns.speaker: ALL
preceding_turns.text: (singing) And I like to do drawings.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Percy has taken one of the boats and placed it as a guard. Keyleth stands here. You guys are way off at the treeline.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Oh god! I killed Percy! There we go. And Vax, you're off that side. What you're seeing is this swirling torrent of broken glass with these two air elementals encroaching on the sides there.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Percy, you're going to need a bigger boat. (laughter)

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Everybody sees everybody right now. I need you all to roll initiative. (groaning)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As the ringing subsides from the blast, you all were thrown dozens of feet from your current placement, most of you onto your back aside from you, Vax, and you, Grog. Actually, Trinket would have caught himself as well. You now see a giant crater in the ground with rock and rubble and shards of glass tossed around you. The trees in the center have been blasted out, and you can see a number of broken trunks and roots curved up with little bits of fire and flame burning off their edge, what little bit of burnable material still remained on some of the living trees. The smell of heavy gunpowder recently burned sits in the air as the giant cloud of smoke rises up in the center of the jungle. At this point in time, the goliath has stepped forward from the brush, as well as two other figures off to the side wielding long-barreled rifle-like weapons. There are a few other figures on this side, as well.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And actually, you don't see that one. And Ripley steps out of the bushes from the side.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The opposite side. This is a heavy groove in the ground. These are the only parts of the land that still stand at normal ground height. The rest of this is a giant slope that goes into a crevasse and rises back up.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Vegetation in that area is completely gone, and there is broken rock and glass everywhere.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's about 20 feet deep at its deepest.
